mechanism_narrative: Declining UNRATE indicates improving labor market conditions which boost consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the negative correlation between UNRATE monthly changes and forward 20-day returns is not statistically significant (one-tailed t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation exceeding 2.5% forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return after CPI YoY > 2.5% is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p>0.1) or if the trigger produces positive returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When Fed funds rate is above its 12-month historical average and industrial production is contracting month-over-month, tight monetary policy coincides with weakening real activity, a classic late-cycle risk that depresses equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > MA12(FEDFUNDS) and INDPRO MoM change < 0
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS_high * INDPRO_decline) in a logit model predicting negative returns is not significant (p>0.1) or if the joint signal does not improve over individual signals in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by very low unemployment and above-trend GDP growth, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal potential oversupply and overheating that leads to a correction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, contrary to the early-cycle positive relationship.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 4.5% and GDPC1 YoY growth > 2.5%)
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST changes and forward 60-day returns is not significantly more negative in the defined late-cycle regime compared to early-cycle (z-test on Fisher-transformed correlations p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 signal strong underlying labor demand and economic momentum, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the mean forward 5-day return after strong payroll prints is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p>0.1) or if the strategy's Sharpe ratio is negative over any two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth reflects broad-based demand expansion, which drives corporate profits and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between GDPC1 annualized growth and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 10-year rolling period, or if the strategy produces negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) mutually reinforce consumer spending power, creating a virtuous demand cycle that pushes stock prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: If the joint trigger does not yield a significantly higher mean return than either trigger alone (ANOVA with contrast p>0.1), or if the strategy's information ratio is below 0.3 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal an imminent manufacturing slowdown or recession, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives equity prices lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return following the signal is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p>0.1) or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.
